MonoBehaviour (fileID 764083142, guid 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77) matches the provided AttachToElevator script on GameObject 'platform2 (1)'. Current scene config: BoxCollider (id 764083144) m_IsTrigger=0; Has_Rigidbody=False; MonoBehaviour.player is null (fileID 0). The script only exposes Unity messages OnTriggerEnter(Collider) and OnTriggerExit(Collider) and no public no-arg methods. Therefore, no manual Trigger calls are available. Also, OnTrigger* cannot be fired automatically because this object is not a trigger and neither collider in the interaction is guaranteed to have a Rigidbody. Even if triggers fired, the internal condition if (gameObject == player) would not execute because 'player' is null. To test and cover: we need either (a) BoxCollider.m_IsTrigger=1 and a Rigidbody on one of the interacting objects plus a valid 'player' reference, or (b) a public no-arg test hook method to invoke, or (c) fix the comparison to other.gameObject == player.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 2013310826, guid: 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77) matches script AttachToElevator on platform2 (2). To exercise OnTriggerEnter/OnTriggerExit, BoxCollider (id: 2013310828) must have m_IsTrigger=1 and the 'player' field must reference the Player GameObject in the scene. Currently, m_IsTrigger=0 and 'player' is null, so triggers will never fire. After setting m_IsTrigger=1 and assigning a valid Player object, move the Player into and out of the platform's trigger volume to automatically invoke OnTriggerEnter/Exit. Note: The script compares 'gameObject == player' instead of 'other.gameObject == player'; unless 'player' is set to platform2 (2) itself (which results in a no-op parent operation), the intended parenting of the Player will not occur. This needs confirmation or fix to validate expected behavior.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 666190386, guid: 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77) matches script AttachToElevator. Scene serialization shows player: {fileID: 0} (null). BoxCollider (id: 666190388) is present with m_IsTrigger: 0 and the GameObject has no Rigidbody. The script only has private OnTriggerEnter/OnTriggerExit and no public methods. Intended behavior: when the Player enters the elevator collider, set Player as child of platform2 (3); when exiting, detach. To trigger these conditions, the collider must be set to IsTrigger=1 and 'player' must reference the Player object. Note: current code compares 'gameObject == player' instead of 'other.gameObject == player', so even with correct setup, it will not parent the Player unless 'player' is the same GameObject as platform2 (3), which is incorrect. There are no m_Calls (Interactable events) defined for this MonoBehaviour in the scene meta.

Exercise AttachToElevator.OnTriggerEnter on platform2. Preconditions: BoxCollider (id 397541586) must be set to m_IsTrigger=1, and either platform2 or the Player must have a Rigidbody. The attached MonoBehaviour component (id 397541585; script guid 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77; field 'player' currently null) must have its 'player' reference set to the Player GameObject. When the Player's collider enters platform2's trigger, OnTriggerEnter should run. Note: current code checks if (gameObject == player); this will only execute if 'player' references platform2 itself, which likely prevents coverage of SetParent unless misconfigured and may cause invalid self-parenting.

Exercise AttachToElevator.OnTriggerExit on platform2. Same preconditions as OnTriggerEnter. After the Player leaves platform2's trigger, OnTriggerExit should run and clear the player's parent (player.transform.parent = null). Note: due to the code's condition (gameObject == player), this will only execute if 'player' references platform2 itself.
